items.title: Convolutional image models
items.description: CNNs for classification, detection, and segmentation apply ReLU or Leaky ReLU after conv layers to introduce nonlinearity while keeping training fast and gradients healthy on positive activations.

items.title: Multilayer perceptrons for tabular and embedding tasks
items.description: MLP hidden layers in recommendation, ranking, and small-scale prediction pipelines use ReLU as the default activation between linear layers.

items.title: Generative adversarial networks (discriminator and generator)
items.description: Many GAN architectures use Leaky ReLU in the discriminator (and sometimes generator) to reduce dead neurons and stabilize adversarial training.

items.title: Efficient on-device inference
items.description: ReLU's threshold operation maps cleanly to hardware and quantized kernels—cheaper than exp-based activations like sigmoid or GELU at inference time.
